title: Repair or replace broken Bitwarden CLI runtime
goal: Repair or replace the current crashing Bitwarden CLI path so the infra safe-status probe can pass without reading or leaking secrets, while preserving rollback evidence and avoiding any unlock/session output in logs.

acceptance.assertion: A replacement path is selected from a verified native Bitwarden CLI binary, a working Flatpak CLI wrapper, or a documented Node/npm package path, with source, version and checksum evidence.
scripts/bitwarden/bw_status_safe.py succeeds against the selected CLI without printing raw Bitwarden output, email, UUID, BW_SESSION or vault item data.
scripts/bitwarden/bw_status_safe.py --require-unlocked fails closed when the vault is not unlocked and does not reveal session or secret material.
The previous symlink target and sha256 are recorded and there is a rollback or disable path if the replacement CLI regresses.

metadata.source: Follow-up from OPERATOR-TOOLING-FOCUS-V1-T003 / infra PR #104 live finding.
metadata.live_blocker: /home/alex/.local/bin/bw -> /home/alex/.local/bin/bw-bin/bw-nodefree crashes with signal 5/Trace-Breakpoint for bw --version and bw status.
metadata.outcome: Implemented in infra PR #105: the active bw path now resolves to a wrapper that runs @bitwarden/cli@2026.6.0 in a transient user systemd scope, avoiding the Grabowski service executable-memory restriction. The wrapper blocks command-line --session use and the safe-status probe passes while the vault remains locked.
validation: Infra PR #105 GitHub CI passed validate.
Local before merge: make lint-bitwarden and make validate passed in /home/alex/repos/.infra-worktrees/bitwarden-cli-runtime-repair-v1.
Live runtime after repair: bw --version returned 2026.6.0; bw status returned status locked with redacted identity material.

Post-merge on infra main 100579d0b5fce6c1cd3623c8492bf55a12d7b16c: scripts/bitwarden/bw_status_safe.py returned rc=0 ok true, vault_status locked, does_not_read_vault_items true, prints_session_key false; --require-unlocked returned rc=2 reason vault-not-unlocked.
Wrapper command-line session-key guard: bw --session dummy status returned rc=64.

verified_acceptance.candidate-selected: Selected @bitwarden/cli@2026.6.0 from npm with recorded package integrity and installed it behind /home/alex/.local/bin/bw-bin/bw-systemd-run-npm-2026.6.0. Native Linux binary and direct Node/npm execution were rejected because they crashed under the Grabowski service execution boundary.
verified_acceptance.safe-status-green: scripts/bitwarden/bw_status_safe.py passes against the selected wrapper with ok true, version 2026.6.0 and vault_status locked. It prints no raw Bitwarden output and does not read vault items.
verified_acceptance.require-unlocked-fail-closed: scripts/bitwarden/bw_status_safe.py --require-unlocked returns rc=2 with reason vault-not-unlocked while the vault is locked, without revealing session or secret material.
verified_acceptance.rollback-or-disable: Previous broken native target /home/alex/.local/bin/bw-bin/bw-nodefree and sha256 c02d90efef5feefdfec8bb452bb8ee5d2af9dd7de175a9a258d11cb1002b2e42 are recorded. The installer records old/new link targets and hashes and can relink or disable the wrapper if it regresses.
